symptoms.symptom_name: Hesitancy with delayed initiation of micturition and straining to void

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hesitancy and straining are core obstructive voiding symptoms of bladder-outlet obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weak, intermittent urinary stream with terminal dribbling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weak intermittent flow is a hallmark obstructive symptom of prostatic outflow resistance.

snomed_ref.display: Feeling of incomplete emptying of bladder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects elevated post-void residual from incomplete bladder emptying against obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy nocturia (eight to ten times per night) with sleep deprivation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturia is a common storage symptom in BPH; its severity here drives the falls-risk and quality-of-life impact.
Alpha-agonist decongestants increase prostatic/bladder-neck smooth-muscle tone and are a well-recognised reversible precipitant of acute-on-chronic outflow obstruction and retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still passing small volumes of urine (no complete retention)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued voiding of small volumes argues against complete acute retention, though near-retention remains and needs quantification.
Absence of fever, dysuria and prostatic tenderness argues strongly against infective prostatitis / UTI.
Absence of visible haematuria is reassuring but does not exclude malignancy; PSA and DRE remain the assessment pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrically enlarged, smooth, firm prostate with obliterated median sulcus and no nodules on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A smooth, symmetrical, non-nodular enlarged prostate favours benign hyperplasia over malignancy — but this is a digital rectal examination finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Absence of a hard, irregular nodule or asymmetry makes malignancy less likely, though PSA and specialist assessment still apply.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full but non-tender suprapubic area with a palpable bladder edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable bladder indicates significant residual volume / near-retention — a palpation finding unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-void residual 250 mL on bedside bladder scan

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated residual (250 mL) consistent with incomplete emptying / near-retention
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without quantifying the residual, progression to complete retention and obstructive renal impairment can be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively confirms bladder-outlet obstruction with significant residual — a bedside-ultrasound measurement the patient cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maximum urinary flow rate less than 15 mL/s on uroflowmetry

investigation_details.expected_result: Weak, intermittent flow with maximum flow rate below 15 mL/s
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Formal flow assessment is a specialist adjunct; not required acutely but supports obstruction.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reduced maximum flow rate objectively supports bladder-outlet obstruction; requires clinic equipment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Hesitancy, weak intermittent stream, terminal dribbling and incomplete emptying together form the classic obstructive LUTS pattern of prostatic outflow obstruction.
A newly-started alpha-agonist decongestant plus a rising post-void residual identifies the drug-precipitated acute exacerbation whose reversal is the priority intervention.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies retention risk that cannot be assessed remotely; drives whether catheterisation is needed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Digital rectal examination of the prostate (in-person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses prostate size, consistency and nodularity — cannot be done via telehealth.
Excludes infection and haematuria contributing to the picture.
Detects obstructive renal impairment from an elevated residual; baseline before any therapy.
Baseline prostate assessment in an older man with new obstructive LUTS; interpret in context and after counselling.
